marketState: neutral-to-bearish medium term, with near-term bounce risk
summary: Front (Aug26) sits almost exactly at modeled fair value ($2.917) across all horizons out to +21D, so there is no cheap/rich mispricing to lean on. The medium-term tilt is bearish: price is running well below its seasonal median (-14.3% YTD vs -3.4% median), the 16-year seasonality sample shows only a 31% chance of being higher in 4 weeks with a typical -3.1% move, storage is in a +184 Bcf 5y surplus with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build, and daily/weekly technicals trend bearish. Near term, 1-7 day weather demand is running hot (+19.9% vs 5y, near the top of its range) which should support the front contract even as 8-16 day demand fades back toward/below normal (-3.2% vs 5y). Daily RSI (35.6) and a close below the lower daily Bollinger band suggest the recent slide is stretched and vulnerable to a short-covering bounce, while managed money is net short but only at the 35th percentile of the 5y range, so the short isn't crowded enough to expect a violent squeeze. The futures curve carries a large, normal winter risk premium (Jan27 near $4.24) rather than any unusual signal.

longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 0.5-1% account risk (low conviction, wide seasonality confidence interval)
longTerm.rationale: Seasonality is running well below its median with a negative typical 4-week move and low reliability, storage is in a growing 5y surplus with a bearish weekly surprise, and 8-16D weather demand is fading back toward normal — all pointing to further downside pressure over the next several weeks. Fair value is 'FAIR' at every horizon (no rich signal), so this is a moderate seasonal/fundamental short rather than a valuation short.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained heat build in the 8-16D outlook, a bullish (below-forecast) storage build, or a weekly close back above ~$3.15 would invalidate the bearish case.

shortTerm.rationale: 1-7 day weather demand is running hot (+19.9% vs 5y, rangePos 0.89) and price is fair-valued, while the daily chart is oversold (RSI 35.6, closed below the lower Bollinger band) with 5m/15m/1h timeframes turning neutral-to-bullish, arguing for a short-covering bounce even inside a larger bearish trend. The front trading at a slight premium to the next month also supports near-term tightness.
shortTerm.invalidation: A break and daily close below $2.75, or a sharp fade in the 1-7D heat forecast, would invalidate the bounce thesis.
